description: RESOLUTIONS Adopted ut the lute meeting of the New York State Anti Slatciy Society 1 Resolved That the principles which are the soul of the untislavcry enterprise comprehend in their natural application authoritative demands and healthful influ ences in all human relations 2 That our integrity dignity and use fulness individually our unanimity strength nnd success socially vitally de pend upon our honoring these principles in all our aims plans and endeavors 3 That as in man the moral is the foun dation to which every part of the human constitution is adjusted so in all our ex i crlions to improve the character and con dition of man and especially to elevate the slavo to the position of freedom we will under God make moral suasiou the basis of our endeavors i 4 That wc can give our countenance and support to such religious teachers alone as heartily and earnestly labor to se cure for the slave his inalienable rights and Godgiven prerogatives 5 That tho Liberty party has the strong est claims to the sympathy and support of all the frrends of freedom and that all who have access to the ballot box ought to express that sympathy
